google-devfest.text: Biggest hands-on workshop he'd attended; took the mic in front of 500+ and deployed an ML Gmail add-on with Gemini.

uofthacks-13.text: Theme 'Identity'. Built Big Brother, a Chrome extension helping older adults navigate websites by voice (RAG, multiple AI models).

deltahacks.text: Built SkySync, analyzing 1.6M+ flights into fuel-saving formation pairs on a 3D globe. 600+ hackers; first time at McMaster.

hack-canada-2026.text: Built ERoute and won the Google Build with AI track at Hack Canada.

utra-hacks-2026.text: Robotics-focused hackathon at UofT; team won the MLH Best Use of ElevenLabs prize.

qhacks.text: Queen's University hackathon in Kingston; led into the Kingston Mayor's Innovation Challenge.

kingston-mayors-innovation-challenge.text: Continuation of QHacks; traveled back to Kingston to pitch a 3D urban-planning tool to the Mayor at City Hall.

hack-the-globe.text: Global Spark social-impact hackathon; built a platform for people with hearing disabilities to practice interviews in sign language.

deerhacks-v.text: 36-hour hackathon at UTM; submitted Hand Tiles.

stupid-ideas-hackathon.text: A deliberately absurd hackathon; won a prize with the project 'It's still 2016'.

smilehacks-2026.text: Won 3rd place with Team 1, earning a NEST 2026 (UofT Hatchery) follow-up.

stan-hackai-toronto.text: 48-hour, $20K hackathon with 60 builders; built an AI video editor on Stan's stack after meeting their CTO.

hack-the-future.text: Won 1st place pitching Omni, a supply-chain disruption/risk agent, on stage at the Google office.

sandcastle-sandbox.text: 12-hour Toronto Tech Week hackathon at MottoMotto Cafe (built in 11 days by Akira Takaki & Victoria Chernobay); won Pokemon cards for a cheeky pitch.

nvidia-spark-hack-toronto.text: NVIDIA Spark hackathon during Toronto Tech Week, hosted at OneEleven; great food and arcade games.

innovation-hackathon-makerslounge.text: Berto Mill / Disruptive Edge's well-run Toronto Tech Week innovation hackathon.

first-hackathon.text: His first hackathon — got carried by a 4th-year student and won 2nd place, sparking the whole journey.

soon-hackathon.text: The hackathon Jack founded: ~40-70 builders live, eat, and build in a private mansion for a weekend. Sponsors flown in from SF.

genai-genesis-2026.text: Helped run 'Canada's largest' AI hackathon as Director of Operations — catering, 1000+ name tags, logistics.

codequest.text: UTMSAM orientation event (hedgehog theme), 100+ participants solving Math/CS problems across the UTM campus.

bearhacks-2025.text: Spoke to 70+ hackers about his journey and 'unethical hacks', then returned the next day to judge.

frasershacks-2026.text: Served as a judge at FrasersHacks (location approximate — to confirm).

bell-sap-case-competition.text: Participated in the Bell SAP Case Competition (details to confirm).

cutc-2026.text: Canadian Undergraduate Technology Conference — a leading student tech conference.

devtools-toronto-7.text: Developer-tools meetup (organized by Ben Vinegar) during Toronto Tech Week; met highly technical builders.

schulich-york-party.text: Crashed a Schulich School of Business party, meeting engineers, a DJ, and business owners.

homecoming-ttw.text: The largest TTW event (1100+ guests at HISTORY) — CEOs and founders on stage, free swag and ice cream.

cohere-party.text: Met Toronto Mayor Olivia Chow and Cohere founders Ivan Zhang & Nick Frosst; name engraved into swag.

organizers-meetup.text: Pitched SOON to other hackathon organizers during Toronto Tech Week.

krista-kim-heart-space.text: A Toronto Tech Week art-meets-tech experience by digital artist Krista Kim, with an afterparty.

ai-collective-ontario-hackathons-mixer.text: Toronto Tech Week mixer connecting Ontario hackathon organizers and builders.

google-office-tour.text: 'Most impressive office' — game room, indoor golf, music studios, 3D printing, floors themed on Canadian culture.

microsoft-canada-hq.text: Invited to a project showcase on the 43rd floor of Microsoft's Canadian HQ at CIBC Square.

amazon-fulfillment-center.text: Toured an 850,000-sq-ft fulfillment center with 10,000+ coordinated robots and miles of conveyor belts.

shopify-office-tour.text: Karaoke room, movie theatre, indoor garden, a car inside the office; spoke with VP Farhan Thawar.

deloitte-office-tour.text: Consulting-world tour with green neon stairs and downtown views; chatted on finance, consulting, strategy.

vancouver-solo-trip.text: First solo trip — flew 4,500km to Vancouver for a Halloween party for future founders.

aucctus-ai-internship.text: Joined Aucctus AI as a Software Engineer Intern building agentic systems for Fortune 500 companies.
